channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's Rural Water Access Reaches 85% Coverage
item.description: Tanzania has made significant progress in improving access to clean water in rural areas, with coverage now reaching 85% according to recent reports. This development marks a crucial step toward addre...

item.title: Andy Burnham outlines vision for Labour government and social care reforms ahead of PM role
item.description: Andy Burnham, the newly elected Labour leader, has confirmed he is finalising his cabinet appointments ahead of assuming the role of Prime Minister on Monday. In his first speech as Labour leader, Bur...

item.title: Trump Reacts to Wildfire Smoke Impacting U.S. Air Quality
item.description: President Donald Trump escalated tensions with Canada on July 17, 2026, after wildfires and smoke from the northern neighbor caused significant air quality issues in the U.S. Midwest and Northeast. In...

item.title: Technical Insights from Rebuilding a macOS Gaming Diagnostics App in Swift
item.description: The article details the author's experience in improving a macOS gaming diagnostics tool called CloudBoost. Initially, the app struggled to provide actionable insights into cloud gaming instability is...

item.title: Trump's repeated election fraud allegations threaten democratic integrity ahead of midterms
item.description: US President Donald Trump has used a nationwide address to revive claims the 2020 election was stolen, despite declassified documents refuting these assertions. Critics argue his focus is not on provi...

item.title: Banyana Coach Announces Wafcon Squad, Focuses on Team Harmony and Preparation
item.description: Banyana Banyana coach Desiree Ellis has finalized her 26-player squad for the Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) 2026, emphasizing a harmonious team environment ahead of the tournament. The team, ...

item.title: Supreme Court clears release of animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' after legal dispute
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has permitted the release of the animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' nationwide on or after July 28, following the conclusion of the annual Jagannath Rath Yatra festival. T...

item.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford Addresses Wildfire Response and Evacuations
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford emphasized that no lives have been lost despite 191 active wildfires ravaging the province, calling it a 'miracle' due to the efforts of firefighters, OPP, and paramedics. Th...

item.title: Andy Burnham's rise as UK Prime Minister amid policy uncertainties

item.title: U.S.-Iran Negotiations Stall as Military Escalation Continues
item.description: The U.S. and Iran's 60-day negotiation period to resolve tensions over nuclear programs, sanctions, and freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz has reached its halfway point. Despite initial hop...

item.description: The article discusses a recent musical challenge between two artists, Fyang and JM, titled 'Song Association.' Published on July 18, 2026, the piece highlights their competition in a creative performa...

item.title: Herbert and Burns tie major record with 62 at British Open
item.description: Lucas Herbert and Sam Burns made history at the 154th Open Championship by achieving a record-breaking round of eight-under par 62, tying the lowest score in major golf history. The duo's performance ...

item.title: Understanding seal behaviour through Neil the Seal's unique interactions
item.description: Neil the Seal, a five-year-old southern elephant seal, has become a social media sensation due to his antics at Seven Mile Beach in Tasmania. While his behaviour of lounging on streets and climbing on...

item.title: Zimbabwean women rebuild lives after climate disaster but face ongoing challenges
item.description: The article discusses how women in Tsholotsho, Zimbabwe, were displaced by Cyclone Dineo in 2017, losing homes, farmland, and livelihoods. Despite these losses, women actively worked to support their ...

item.title: Leon Black Testifies About Epstein's Role in Nondisclosure Agreements
item.description: Newly released transcripts from a House Oversight Committee interview reveal billionaire Leon Black's panicked attempts to avoid discussing nondisclosure agreements (NDAs) linked to convicted sex offe...

item.title: education updates: ielts transition, neet ecosystem, and university admissions in july 2026
item.description: the article highlights key developments in the education sector as of july 17, 2026. computer-based ielts will replace paper-based exams in india from september, with paper-based exams ending in augus...

item.title: Cabinet Secretary Abalos Extends Condolences at Motorcycle Rider's Wake After Fatal Attack
item.description: Cabinet Secretary Benjamin 'Benhur' Abalos Jr. attended the wake of slain motorcycle rider Fabio Norberte Jr., extending President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s condolences to his family. Abalos provided fin...

item.title: Governor Radda Urges Katsina Residents to Expose Bandit Informants and Collaborators for Lasting Peace
item.description: Katsina State Governor, Malam Dikko Umaru Radda, has called on residents to expose bandit informants and collaborators, stressing that lasting peace cannot be achieved if criminal networks continue to...

item.title: Dyson Hot+Cool HF1: A Year-Round Smart Appliance Review
item.description: The Dyson Hot+Cool HF1 is a 2-in-1 smart appliance that provides heating in winter and cooling in summer, offering year-round comfort. Priced at $499, it combines Dyson's bladeless design with smart c...

item.title: Tanzania recognizes OSHA's role in rural development
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania's recognition of OSHA's contributions to national development, particularly in improving rural water access. It notes that rural water coverage has risen to 85%, a sign...
